mnemonics: DBSCAN: Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise
Hierarchical: Huge trees Have leaves (clusters) growing from roots (data)
keyPoints: Clustering is an unsupervised learning method for grouping unlabeled data points.
K-Means partitions data into 'k' clusters by minimizing variance within each cluster.
DBSCAN forms clusters based on dense regions of data points and can identify outliers as noise.
Hierarchical clustering builds a tree of clusters either by merging (agglomerative) or splitting (divisive) data points.
commonMistakes: Assuming K-Means can handle non-spherical or arbitrarily shaped clusters effectively.
Forgetting to scale data before using distance-based algorithms like K-Means, which distorts results.
Arbitrarily choosing the number of clusters 'k' without using methods like the elbow method or domain knowledge.
